commitments.text: Brazil will strive to achieve its NDC through domestic measures to be coordinated and implemented by the federal government.
The Brazilian government does not rule out the use of internationally transferred mitigation outcomes (ITMOs) under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement.

measurability.reason: Neither commitment provides a measurable target or year.

gap_findings.description: Brazil's NDC does not make a specific commitment on adaptation finance.
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: [WG2_Chapter17_DecisionMakingRisk_2022, p.48] Adaptation finance gaps persist; developing countries need significantly more than current flows.

gap_findings.description: Brazil's NDC commitment is vague and lacks quantification.
gap_findings.ndc_evidence: Brazil will strive to achieve its NDC through domestic measures to be coordinated and implemented by the federal government.
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: [WG2_Chapter17_DecisionMakingRisk_2022, p.52] Adaptation finance provided by international public mechanisms remains the core source of tracked flows in support of adaptation and resilience to developing countries.

gap_findings.description: Brazil's NDC commitment on ITMOs is conditional on receiving international finance.
gap_findings.ndc_evidence: The Brazilian government does not rule out the use of internationally transferred mitigation outcomes (ITMOs) under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement.
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: [WG2_Chapter17_DecisionMakingRisk_2022, p.48] The '100 Billion' does not represent the total need to respond to climate change in developing countries, nor the global cost across all countries.

details.summary: Brazil's NDC commitments on adaptation finance are incomplete, vague, and conditional on international finance, indicating a significant gap between its commitments and the IPCC benchmark.

The Brazilian government does not rule out the use of internationally transferred mitigation outcomes (ITMOs) under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement.

pass_c.country_summary: Brazil's NDC commitments on adaptation finance are incomplete, vague, and conditional on international finance, indicating a significant gap between its commitments and the IPCC benchmark.
pass_c.policy_recommendation: Brazil should make a specific, quantifiable commitment on adaptation finance and ensure that its commitment is unconditional and aligned with the IPCC's recommendations.
